name: dynamic-review
description: Mutation-capable dynamic review workflow: inspect an applied patch, write targeted repro tests, run narrow validation, and report RED/GREEN evidence.

stages.prompt: You are running a dynamic code review in a disposable workspace where the proposed patch is already applied. Write the smallest targeted repro tests for behavior-changing regressions you can justify from local source evidence. Run only narrow, safe validation commands. Do not perform broad refactors. Do not modify files outside this workspace. If you run a command, record the exact command, exit code, purpose, and concise evidence summary in the final JSON commandRuns array. Prefer RED evidence on patched code; include GREEN evidence only when you can safely obtain it by a minimal revert/fix. If a suspicious hunk is behavior-preserving, record it as a caveat or trapAssessment instead of writing a defect test. Return JSON only with summary, findings, commandRuns, and trapAssessments.

useWhen: write repro tests for a patch
